rulings.Case Title: JANET M. PLANELLS VS. STATE OF CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 7, 2019, Line 4, DEFENDANT HEALTHRIGHT 360, A NONPROFIT CORPORATION MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Summary Adjudication Defendant Healthright 360's motion for summary judgment is continued to June 11, 2019 pursuant to Plaintiff's request under Code of Civil Procedure § 437c(h). The declarations of Fulvio Cajina and Matthew Haley demonstrate just cause for the continuance as they identify specific facts essential to oppose the motion. Dr. Cohen was decedent's main treating therapist and a key witness in this case. Dr. Cohen's deposition occurred on April 22, 2019, which was only one day prior to the opposition due date, and there was insufficient time to incorporate her testimony into the opposition papers. Although the transcript of Dr. Cohen's deposition is attached to the City's reply papers, Plaintiff is entitled to an opportunity to address that testimony. Plaintiff's supplemental opposition is due on May 29, 2019 and the supplemental reply brief is due on June 6, 2019 pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure § 437c. The court further notes that the trial date has been continued to September 9, 2019. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: JEAN KASEM VS. MICHAEL D. HUNTER MD ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: DISCOVERY CALENDAR SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, MAY 7, 2019, LINE 2 DEFENDANT MICHAEL HUNTER, MD'S Motion To Compel Production Of Documents Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: NO OPPOSITION HAS BEEN FILED TO DEFENDANT MICHAEL D. HUNTER'S MOTION TO COMEPL REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS. ACCORDINGLY, THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS FOLLOWS: 1. ALL PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS TO REQUEST NOS 1-19 ARE OVERRULED WITH THE EXCEPTION OF A CLAIM OF ATTORNEY/CLENT PRIVILEGE 2. WITHIN FIFTEEN (15) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER, PLAINTIFF WILL DELIVER TO DEFENDANT MICHAEL D. HUNTERS ALL RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS TO REQUEST NOS 1-19. TO THE EXTENT THAT PLAINTIFF SEEKS TO WITHHOLD ANY RESPONSIVE DOCUMENT CLAIMING ATTORNEY/CLIENT PRIVILEGE, IT WILL IDENTIFY SUCH DOCUMENT WITH SUFFICIENT SPECIFICITY TO ALLOW DEFENDANT TO CHALLENGE THE VALIDITY OF THAT CLAIM. 3. COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT MICHAEL D. HUNTER WILL PREPARE AN APPROPRIATE ORDER, SUBMIT A COPY TO COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND DELIVER A COPY TO THE COURT AT THE HEARING ON MAY 7, 2019. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepar85@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (301/PR)

rulings.Case Title: CREDIT CORP SOLUTIONS INC. VS. SHERMAN LEE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 7, 2019, Line 7, DEFENDANT SHERMAN LEE MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT Defendant Sherman Lee's motion to set aside default and default judgment is granted. Although a proof of service by a registered process server creates a presumption of proper service, the court credits Mr. Lee's uncontroverted declaration that he does not know the person with whom the summons was left and such a person does not live at the address indicated and that Mr. Lee did not receive the summons and complaint. (Lee Decl. 5-7; see Fernandes v. Singh (2017) 16 Cal.App.5th 932, 941["declaration of non-service if credited by the trial court can rebut the presumption of proper service"].) Because service papers were not left with "a competent member of the household" of Mr. Lee and service was thus improper, the default judgment is vacated as void. (See Ellard v. Conway (2001) 94 Cal.App.4th 540, 544 ["Under section 473, subdivision (d), the court may set aside a default judgment which is valid on its face, but void, as a matter of law, due to improper service."].) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: LAWRENCE S. DORFMAN VS. RICHARD CARTER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 7, 2019, Line 14, Plaintiff'S Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication Plaintiff Dorfman's motion for summary adjudication and request for an interlocutory judgment of partition is granted. "'A co-owner of property has an absolute right to partition unless barred by a valid waiver.'" (Orien v. Lutz (2017) 16 Cal.App.5th 957, 962; Code Civ. Proc. § 872.710(b).) "If the court finds that the plaintiff is entitled to partition, it shall make an interlocutory judgment that determines the interests of the parties in the property and orders the partition of the property." (Code Civ. Proc. § 872.720(a).) The Court's full tentative ruling will be e-mailed to the parties. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: NANCY ROMNEY VS. EDWARDS LIFESCIENCES, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 7, 2019, Line 10, DEFENDANT EDWARDS LIFESCIENCES, LLC, EDWARDS LIFESCIENCES CORPORATION, EDWARDS LIFESCIENCES(U.S.)INC. DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Defendants Edwards Lifesciences, LLC, Edwards Lifesciences Corp., and Edwards Lifesciences (U.S.) Inc.'s demurrer to Plaintiff Nancy Romney's first amended complaint is overruled as to the first and second causes of action for strict liability manufacturing defect and negligence in manufacturing and sustained without leave to amend as to the third cause of action for failure to warn. Liberally construing the complaint, Plaintiff has identified federal regulations previously violated by Defendants and alleged to have been violated leading to the incident that injured Plaintiff. (FAC 13, 21, 31.) In addition, the first amended complaint alleges the components of the Sapien 3 that are defective because they were not manufactured consistent with specifications. (Id.) It also alleges that the surgeon who performed Plaintiff's surgery concluded that the balloon was defective as manufactured. (Id. 26.) Finally, it alleges that Defendants were aware of the manufacturing defects prior to Plaintiff's surgery, and recalled the product due to the defect. (Id. 29.) Although these allegations remain fairly general, they are sufficient on demurrer to plead a manufacturing defect claim sufficient to overcome a claim of preemption under the federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act. (See Coleman v. Medtronic, Inc. (2014) 223 Cal.App.4th 413, 436 ["[W]e conclude that it is premature to determine whether Coleman has alleged a state law requirement that is parallel to federal requirements so as to survive preemption. At the pleading stage, we can only conclude that the complaint alleges sufficient facts to state a cause of action for manufacturing defect, the issue of preemption will necessarily be addressed after Coleman has some opportunity to conduct discovery."].) Defendants may raise the issue again by a motion for summary judgment after Plaintiff has had the opportunity to conduct discovery. The Court's full tentative ruling will be e-mailed to all the parties. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)
